free_text: Prescission neutron emission in 235U(nth,f) through
fragment-neutron angular correlation studies
CIRUS reactor at Trombay.
A 235U target of 100 ug/cm2 thickness electrodeposited
on a thin Au (~20 ug/cm2 thick) coated VYNS backing
A back-to-back gridded ionization chamber
to measure the energies of the fission fragment
pair and also the angle of emission with respect to
the electric field direction in chamber.
The ionization chamber consisted of a central cathode
and two parallel plate gridded ionization chambers in
back-to-back geometry with the cathode-grid and
collector-grid distances of 3.0 cm and 0.7 cm,
respectively.
The chamber was filled with P-10 gas at 1.1 atmosphere,
so as to stop all the fission fragments in the
cathode-grid region.
NE213 liquid scintillation neutron detector
(5 cm diam., 5 cm thick) at a distance of 70 cm from
the uranium target, along the direction of the
electric field of the ionization chamber.
The neutron detector was adequately shielded from
background neutrons and gamma rays by using 7-cm
thick lead and 50-cm thick borated paraffin in a
cylindrical geometry.
Time-of-flight by taking the start signal from
the common cathode of the ionization chamber and the
stop signal from the neutron detector.
The time resolution as determined from the prompt
gamma ray peak in the time-of-flight spectrum was
seen to be about 2.5 ns.
The c.m.center-of-mass neutron energy spectra
were determined by analyzing the neutron coincidence
events, corresponding to fragment emission in a cone
of half angle +/-18 deg with respect to the direction
of neutron emission.
The observed neutron angular distributions were
corrected for chance coincidences arising due to
back-ground neutrons.
VT
On. Fainlized.
Neutron - A=140 fragment angle distribution for prompt
fission neutrons emitted with fragments at the total
kinetic energy specified
No information on sources of uncertainties
